message:
docs: update README and porting notes for Cursor provider and CASS parity work

- README.md: Document Cursor as supported provider, update feature list with
  new output format options and deduplication capability
- docs/cass-porting-notes.md: Expand porting notes with Cursor-specific parsing
  details and divergence documentation from original CASS behavior
- .beads/issues.jsonl: Sync issue tracker state after provider and pipeline work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-20x.2","title":"Implement Codex session reader","description":"Implement Provider::read_session() for Codex in src/providers/codex.rs by porting/adapting CASS codex connector behavior.

Format scope:
- Scan rollout files under ~/.codex/sessions (JSONL modern + legacy JSON format support).
- JSONL parsing:
  - session_meta: extract workspace/cwd context
  - response_item: parse primary user/assistant turns
  - event_msg user_message/agent_reasoning/token_count handling with CASS-compatible rules
- Legacy JSON parsing: {session, items} fallback handling.

Critical behavior to preserve:
- Token_count events attach retroactively to latest non-reasoning assistant turn.
- Content flattening covers string and block-array representations.
- External ID derivation preserves relative rollout path identity.
- Unknown/non-conversational events are ignored safely.

Robustness requirements:
- Malformed lines are tolerated; valid messages still parsed.
- Empty/whitespace content turns are skipped.
- Message ordering/indexing remains stable after filtering.

Parity requirements:
- Match CASS codex connector behavior unless intentional divergence is documented.
- Record divergences in docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reader passes synthetic + redacted-real Codex fixture tests.
- Reader passes CASS parity regression tests for Codex fixtures.
- Token usage attachment and legacy-format handling are explicitly unit-tested with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:40:41.242903383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:11.187557755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:11.187534391Z","close_reason":"Implemented Codex JSONL+legacy JSON reader.  {"id":"bd-20x.3","title":"Implement Gemini CLI session reader","description":"Implement Provider::read_session() for Gemini in src/providers/gemini.rs by porting/adapting CASS gemini connector behavior.

Format scope:
- Scan session-*.json files in ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/ only.
- Parse top-level session metadata (sessionId, projectHash, start/lastUpdated).
- Parse messages array with Gemini type semantics (user/model -> user/assistant canonical roles).
- Preserve grounding/citation metadata in message extras.

Workspace inference requirements:
- Use CASS-derived extraction cascade from message content (AGENTS header, working-directory pattern, /data/projects heuristic).
- Normalize Unix/Windows path forms robustly.
- Fall back to hash-directory parent when content extraction fails.

Robustness requirements:
- Tolerate malformed files/records without crashing.
- Skip empty/whitespace message content.
- Maintain stable message order and indexing.

Parity requirements:
- Match CASS gemini connector behavior unless intentional divergence is documented.
- Record divergences in docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reader passes synthetic + redacted-real Gemini fixture tests.
- Reader passes CASS parity regression tests for Gemini fixtures.
- Workspace inference edge cases (quoted paths, Windows paths, truncated project paths) are covered by unit tests with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:40:52.277115849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:14.654942020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:58:14.654905222Z","close_reason":"Implemented Gemini JSON reader.  {"id":"bd-20x.4","title":"Add support for additional providers (Cline, Amp, Aider, Cursor)","description":"After the initial three providers (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) are complete and stable, extend casr to additional providers where formats are known or can be reverse-engineered.

Candidate providers (priority order):
1. Cursor (VS Code globalStorage)
2. Cline (VS Code globalStorage)
3. Aider (Markdown history format)
4. Amp (VS Code + local share paths)
5. OpenCode (SQLite-backed sessions)

Architecture and dependency policy:
- Implement each as a native casr provider module via the same `Provider` trait.
- Port/adapt CASS insights by vendoring logic as needed.
- Do NOT add runtime dependency on CASS.

Quality bar per provider:
- Reader implementation with fixture coverage.
- Writer implementation that produces provider-plausible artifacts.
- `owns_session` discovery support.
- Resume command wiring and CLI integration.
- Round-trip + e2e coverage with detailed logs.
- Document known lossy conversions and unsupported fields.

Execution strategy:
- Ship providers incrementally behind clear task boundaries and tests.
- Do not merge a provider without passing provider-specific reader/writer + e2e smoke coverage.

## Success Criteria

- Each added provider has:
  - detection + ownership lookup
  - reader + writer
  - resume command wiring
  - fixtures + unit tests + round-trip tests + e2e coverage
- Known lossy fields are explicitly documented and asserted in tests.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:40.707719677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:56:21.796394043Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["future","readers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:15.308089591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:57.923127573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:58.003187886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-20x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-20x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:58.078153512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Required coverage dimensions:
1. Unit tests for each reader and writer.
2. Round-trip invariants (`read->canonical->write->read`) including per-provider and cross-provider paths.

Non-negotiable logging/debuggability requirements:
- Every failing test must surface enough context to debug without reproducing manually.
- e2e scripts must output command, expected/actual, artifact paths, and pass/fail summary.

Key invariant:
For each provider `P`, `read_P(write_P(canonical))` should be equivalent to canonical (modulo documented provider-specific lossy fields).

 {"id":"bd-24z.1","title":"Create test fixtures for each provider format","description":"Create a high-fidelity fixture corpus under tests/fixtures/ for provider readers, writers, round-trip tests, and e2e harnesses.

Fixture strategy (MANDATORY):
1. Synthetic fixtures: minimal and edge-case sessions crafted by hand.
2. Redacted-real fixtures: sanitized snapshots derived from real provider session files so writer/reader behavior is anchored to real-world structures.

Provider-native fixture sets:
- Claude Code: simple, complex (content arrays/tool events), missing-workspace, unicode.
- Codex: modern JSONL, legacy JSON, token_count events, reasoning events.
- Gemini: simple, grounding/citations, missing-workspace inference.
- Cross-cutting edge fixtures: empty/whitespace content, null/missing timestamps, long message bodies, single-sided conversations.

Corpus rules:
- Keep provider fixture files strictly provider-native JSON/JSONL (no inline comment lines).
- Put fixture intent and caveats in sidecar notes files and a manifest (e.g., fixtures_manifest.json).
- For each fixture, include expected canonical summary output used by tests (message count/order/roles, workspace, metadata expectations, known lossy fields).
- Include at least one fixture per provider that exercises malformed-line tolerance without breaking parse of valid remaining messages.

Logging/debuggability requirements:
- Tests consuming fixtures must print fixture ID/path when failing.
- Mismatch reports must include expected vs actual canonical summary artifact paths.

Acceptance criteria:
- Fixture corpus includes both synthetic and redacted-real sets for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini.
- Every fixture has a sidecar expected canonical summary and documented intent.
- No provider-native fixture relies on non-native comment syntax.

-{"id":"bd-24z.14","title":"Implement writer golden-output conformance suite","description":"Add a dedicated writer conformance suite that validates generated provider files against golden, redacted-real fixture expectations.

Why this is required:
Reader<->writer round-trip alone can hide symmetric bugs (writer and reader both wrong in the same way). This suite prevents that by checking writer output against external golden expectations.

Scope:
1. For each provider writer (Claude/Codex/Gemini), generate output from canonical fixtures.
2. Compare generated artifacts to provider-specific golden expectations:
   - required field presence/shape
   - event ordering constraints
   - filename/path conventions
   - timestamp format validity
   - provider-specific metadata presence/absence constraints
3. Use tolerant normalization where IDs/timestamps are expected to differ (with explicit normalization rules).
4. Emit human-readable and machine-readable diffs on mismatch.

Logging/artifacts:
- Save generated output, normalized output, and diff artifacts per test case.
- Print fixture ID, provider, and exact mismatch path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Conformance tests cover all three initial providers.
- Symmetric-bug protection is demonstrated (at least one negative fixture/assertion case).
- CI runs conformance suite and publishes mismatch artifacts on failure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:09:04.195576364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:09:04.195576364Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:09:04.195576364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:09:04.195576364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.14","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:09:04.195576364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.14","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:09:04.195576364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.14","depends_on_id":"bd-24z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:09:04.195576364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-24z.15","title":"Implement discovery/reader scalability regression suite","description":"Add performance and scalability regression tests for discovery + parsing on large session corpora.

Scope:
1. Generate deterministic synthetic corpora with high file counts (e.g., 1k/5k/10k mixed provider files).
2. Benchmark key hot paths:
   - provider detection and root scanning
   - owns_session lookup fast-path/fallback
   - reader parse throughput per provider
3. Define budget thresholds for CI perf-regression job (with clear pass/fail criteria).
4. Capture perf diagnostics:
   - files scanned
   - parse count
   - elapsed ms per stage
   - memory usage proxy metrics where practical

Requirements:
- Tests must be deterministic and runnable in CI without accessing real user homes.
- Results must be emitted in machine-readable form for trend tracking.
- Regressions should produce actionable output pinpointing the slow stage.

Acceptance criteria:
- Performance suite covers discovery and all three initial readers.
- CI enforces baseline budgets with documented thresholds.

 {"id":"bd-24z.3","title":"Implement unit tests for each writer","description":"Write comprehensive unit tests for each provider's `write_session()` implementation.

## Primary Strategy: Reader↔Writer Compatibility

For each provider writer:
1. Create a `CanonicalSession` programmatically (known values).
2. Write it using the provider's `write_session()` into a temp provider home.
3. Read it back using the SAME provider's `read_session()`.
4. Compare the original canonical input with the re-read version.
5. Verify message count/order/roles/content/timestamps match (modulo documented lossy fields).

This proves the writer produces files that casr can read back (and is a strong proxy for provider plausibility).

## Secondary Tests

- Output placement: file is written under the correct provider directory structure.
- JSON validity: JSONL lines are valid JSON; JSON files parse.
- Required fields: writer outputs contain provider-required keys/shapes (conformance suite is the authority).
- Timestamp serialization: verify timestamps are in the provider-accepted format.
  - Do NOT assume ISO-8601 for every provider; use per-provider golden expectations + reverse-engineering findings.
- Role mapping rules (assistant/user/tool/system) are correct per provider schema.
- Directory creation: parents created as needed.
- Idempotency: writing the same session twice with `--force` is deterministic after applying normalization rules for generated ids/timestamps.
- Conflict detection: error when target exists without `--force`.

## Logging Requirements

- Log output file path(s) and byte counts.
- On failure, dump written file contents (with line numbers for JSONL).
- Use descriptive `assert_eq!` messages that identify provider + field + line/index.

Use temp dirs (`tempfile`) for isolation; never touch real provider directories.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Writer unit tests exist for Claude/Codex/Gemini and are parallel-safe.
- Writer tests validate both read-back compatibility and provider-specific output shape.
- Failures include full artifacts/logs sufficient for debugging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:43:16.156134049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:46:24.544517032Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:46:24.544421924Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:08.330742642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:08.404474629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:08.478050154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.3","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:12.656407922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.3","depends_on_id":"bd-24z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:08.248529169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-24z.4","title":"Implement round-trip fidelity tests","description":"Write round-trip tests verifying: `read_P(write_P(read_Q(source))) ≈ read_Q(source)` for all provider pairs.

Test all 6 conversion paths:
1. CC→Cod, 2. CC→Gmi, 3. Cod→CC, 4. Cod→Gmi, 5. Gmi→CC, 6. Gmi→Cod.

For each path:
- read source fixture → canonical → write to target format (temp dir) → read back → compare.

Fidelity matrix (expected preservation per field):

| Field              | CC→Cod | CC→Gmi | Cod→CC | Cod→Gmi | Gmi→CC | Gmi→Cod |
|--------------------|--------|--------|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| message_count      | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT   | EXACT  | EXACT   |
| message_roles      | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT   | EXACT  | EXACT   |
| message_content    | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT   | EXACT  | EXACT   |
| timestamps (±1s)   | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT   | EXACT  | EXACT   |
| workspace          | EXACT  | BEST*  | EXACT  | BEST*   | BEST*  | BEST*   |
| session_id         | NEW    | NEW    | NEW    | NEW     | NEW    | NEW     |
| model_name         | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT  | EXACT   | EXACT  | EXACT   |
| git_branch (CC)    | LOST   | LOST   | N/A    | N/A     | N/A    | N/A     |
| token_usage (Cod)  | N/A    | N/A    | LOST   | LOST    | N/A    | N/A     |
| citations (Gmi)    | N/A    | N/A    | N/A    | N/A     | LOST   | LOST    |

EXACT = must match. NEW = new id generated (verify valid format). BEST* = best-effort. LOST = expected loss (document explicitly in test comments). N/A = not present in source.

Logging requirements:
- For each path, log: source_provider, target_provider, message_count.
- On mismatch, show side-by-side message diffs (index, role, content prefix, timestamp delta).
- Print a summary table for all 6 paths.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 6 conversion paths are covered and deterministic.
- Mismatch output is high-signal and includes enough context to debug.

-{"id":"bd-24z.9","title":"Implement unit tests for conversion pipeline with mock providers","description":"Write unit tests for the `ConversionPipeline` using mock providers. Since the pipeline is generic over the `Provider` trait, we can test it with in-memory mock providers that do not touch the filesystem.

## Mock Provider Harness

Create a `MockProvider` that implements `Provider`:
- `detect()` returns configurable `DetectionResult` (binary present/missing; roots readable/writable).
- `owns_session(id)` returns Some(path) for a predefined set.
- `read_session(path)` returns a hardcoded `CanonicalSession` or a controlled error.
- `write_session(session, opts)` records writes and returns `WrittenSession` with fake paths + optional backups.
- `resume_command(id)` returns a formatted string.

Also add a mock resolver hook or deterministic registry layout so bd-3my.2 behaviors (unique/ambiguous/not-found) can be exercised without touching disk.

## Pipeline Tests

Happy paths:
1. Convert from MockA to MockB succeeds; `write_session()` called with expected canonical session.
2. Dry run: `dry_run=true` does not call `write_session()`.
3. Same-provider shortcut: source==target returns early with resume command and no writes.

Discovery/error paths:
4. Session not found: returns `SessionNotFound` with diagnostics.
5. Ambiguous session id: returns `AmbiguousSessionId` and never reads/writes.
6. Target not writable: returns `ProviderUnavailable` (or equivalent) and never writes.
7. Target binary missing but writable: conversion succeeds and emits a WARNING in result.
8. Source hint by alias narrows provider search.
9. Source hint by path bypasses discovery.

Validation coverage (MUST cover ALL checks from bd-ikb.2):
10. Empty session -> `ValidationError`.
11. User-only -> `ValidationError`.
12. Assistant-only -> `ValidationError`.
13. No workspace -> warning (pipeline continues).
14. No timestamps -> warning.
15. Consecutive same-role messages -> warning.
16. Unmatched tool_result.call_id -> info (verbose/trace only).

Verify/rollback:
17. Verify-after-write success path.
18. Verify failure triggers rollback:
    - if backups were created, restore them,
    - remove broken newly written output,
    - return `VerifyFailed`.

## Logging Assertions

Use `tracing-test` (or equivalent) to capture logs in tests.
- Assert key trace messages appear: source resolved, write started, verify passed/failed, rollback actions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Pipeline tests cover all branches and are deterministic.
- Logs emitted by pipeline are asserted for key stages.
- Verify-failure rollback invariants are asserted (no leftover "new" output when failing).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:17.545739660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T15:53:40.986020044Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pipeline","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:11.044946233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:57.759639003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:21.924294875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:07:22.004626263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Types

 {"id":"bd-2rl.2","title":"Define Provider trait with read/write/detect methods","description":"Define the Provider trait in `src/providers/mod.rs`. This is the core abstraction that each provider (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and future providers) implements.

## Trait Surface

```rust
pub trait Provider {
    /// Human-readable name (e.g. "Claude Code")
    fn name(&self) -> &str;

    /// Stable slug used in logs/metadata (e.g. "claude-code", "codex", "gemini")
    fn slug(&self) -> &str;

    /// CLI alias used in `casr <alias> resume ...` (e.g. "cc", "cod", "gmi")
    fn cli_alias(&self) -> &str;

    /// Detect presence of the provider binary + session roots.
    /// This MUST be evidence-rich (not a single bool).
    fn detect(&self) -> DetectionResult;

    /// Candidate session roots for this provider (respects *_HOME overrides).
    fn session_roots(&self) -> Vec<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Fast ownership check: return the native session file path if this provider owns `session_id`.
    /// Should avoid full file parsing when possible.
    fn owns_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Read a session from native format into canonical IR.
    fn read_session(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> anyhow::Result<CanonicalSession>;

    /// Write a canonical session into this provider's native format.
    /// Returns all paths written + generated session_id.
    fn write_session(&self, session: &CanonicalSession, opts: &WriteOptions)
        -> anyhow::Result<WrittenSession>;

    /// The resume command string for this provider (e.g. `claude --resume <id>`).
    fn resume_command(&self, session_id: &str) -> String;
}
```

Also define:

```rust
pub struct DetectionResult {
    pub binary: Option<BinaryEvidence>,
    pub roots: Vec<RootEvidence>,
    pub notes: Vec<String>,
}

pub struct BinaryEvidence {
    pub exe: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub version: Option<String>,
}

pub struct RootEvidence {
    pub path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub exists: bool,
    pub readable: bool,
    pub writable: bool,
    pub evidence: Vec<String>,
}

impl DetectionResult {
    pub fn can_read(&self) -> bool { /* any readable root */ }
    pub fn can_write(&self) -> bool { /* any writable root OR creatable root */ }
    pub fn can_resume(&self) -> bool { self.binary.is_some() }
}

pub struct WrittenSession {
    pub paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
    pub session_id: String,
    pub resume_command: String,
    pub backup_paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
}

pub struct WriteOptions {
    pub force: bool,
}
```

## Detection Semantics (Critical For UX)

- `detect()` MUST distinguish:
  - binary present vs missing (`can_resume()`),
  - session roots present/readable (`can_read()`),
  - session roots writable/creatable (`can_write()`).
- casr should be able to READ sessions even when the provider binary is not installed.
- casr should be able to WRITE sessions as long as roots are writable/creatable, even if the binary is missing (warn in UX).

## Conflict + Backup Policy

- Conflict/`--force` handling MUST live in exactly one place: `atomic_write` (bd-ikb.3).
- Provider writers call `atomic_write`; the pipeline should not duplicate pre-checks (avoid TOCTOU).
- `WrittenSession.backup_paths` is surfaced to UX so users can find restored/overwritten files.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `Provider` is object-safe and storable as `Box<dyn Provider>` in a registry.
- `DetectionResult` is rich enough to power `casr providers` output (binary evidence + root evidence + notes).
- Env var overrides are supported for root derivation: `CLAUDE_HOME`, `CODEX_HOME`, `GEMINI_HOME`.
- `write_session()` uses `WriteOptions.force` and surfaces backup paths when overwriting.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:32.295047628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:53.161795562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:53.161775054Z","close_reason":"Provider trait already defined in src/providers/mod.rs: 9 methods (name, slug, cli_alias, detect, session_roots, owns_session, read_session, write_session, resume_command).
